rationale: Both papers are centrally about consumer choice modeling for bundles: the cited paper develops a general bundle choice model (COBA) for heterogeneous multi-category bundles, while the citing paper develops adapted incidence/quantity/choice models for bundle promotions and explicitly positions itself as extending prior bundling research. Given this strong conceptual/method overlap and an exact DOI match, the citation could plausibly be a foundational theory/method reference rather than an incidental mention.
